name: Dark dimension swampland + relaxion + type-I seesaw
summary: Classification: ACTIVE RECENT PROGRAM (2022+). Motivated by the swampland distance conjecture: a single extra dimension of size R ~ micrometer sets a Kaluza-Klein scale m_KK ~ R^-1 ~ 100 meV, naturally identified with the dark-energy scale (Lambda^{1/4}). KK gravitons in the dark dimension are then dark-matter candidates with mass-mixing controlled by the same scale. The relaxion is added to address the electroweak hierarchy via cosmological relaxation rather than symmetry. Bulk right-handed neutrinos in the dark dimension give the type-I seesaw with the seesaw scale geometrically explained. Distinguished from old large-extra-dimensions programs by being motivated top-down from string-theory swampland constraints, and by the parametric identification Lambda^{1/4} ~ m_KK ~ m_nu rather than independent scales. The framework is young (Montero-Vafa-Valenzuela 2022) and not yet pressured by collider data.

lineage.citation: Montero, Vafa, Valenzuela, 'The dark dimension and the Swampland', JHEP 02 (2023) 022
Anchordoqui, Antoniadis, Lust, 'Dark dimension, the swampland, and the origin of cosmic acceleration', PRD 107, 083530 (2023)
Gonzalo, Montero, Obied, Vafa, 'Dark dimension gravitons as dark matter', JHEP 11 (2023) 109
Graham, Kaplan, Rajendran, 'Cosmological relaxation of the electroweak scale', PRL 115, 221801 (2015)
